title: Huberantha sambiranensis
intro: Huberantha sambiranensis, also known as the Madagascar berry bush or Madagascan plum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Madagascar for its medicinal properties, including its potential to treat fever, coughs, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Huberantha sambiranensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
